inline_comments.message: Would it not work, if in existing index we added anchor as well ?

The unique index on dataspace, anchor and xpath is already defined. This is `fragment_dataspace_id_anchor_id_xpath_key`

Should we be get rid of UQ_FRAGMENT_XPATH instead of recreating it non unique ?

Toine, could you please have dba feedback on this ?

If that is already defined than could you please check without having this non unique index it should give same performance AND Unique_index for xpath, dataspace_id and anchor should be used (can be checked using explain statement). It looks redundant overhead on db to create this index then.
The fragment_dataspace_id_anchor_id_xpath_key is a constraint on the table and not an index, whereas the actual index UQ_FRAGMENT_XPATH is missing the anchor.

`fragment_dataspace_id_anchor_id_xpath_key` constraint is also implemented as an index:


 cpsdb=# \d fragment
                               Table "public.fragment"
      Column     |  Type   | Collation | Nullable |             Default
 ----------------+---------+-----------+----------+----------------------------------
  id             | bigint  |           | not null | generated by default as identity
  xpath          | text    |           | not null |
  attributes     | jsonb   |           |          |
  anchor_id      | bigint  |           |          |
  parent_id      | bigint  |           |          |
  dataspace_id   | integer |           | not null |
  schema_node_id | integer |           |          |
 Indexes:
     "fragment_pkey" PRIMARY KEY, btree (id)
     "fki_fragment_anchor_id_fk" btree (anchor_id)
     "fki_fragment_dataspace_id_fk" btree (dataspace_id)
     "fki_fragment_parent_id_fk" btree (parent_id)
     "fki_schema_node_id_to_id" btree (schema_node_id)
     "fragment_dataspace_id_anchor_id_xpath_key" UNIQUE CONSTRAINT, btree (dataspace_id,  anchor_id, xpath)
     "uq_fragment_xpath" UNIQUE, btree (xpath, dataspace_id)
 Foreign-key constraints:
     "fragment_anchor_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (anchor_id) REFERENCES anchor(id)
     "fragment_dataspace_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (dataspace_id) REFERENCES dataspace(id)
     "fragment_parent_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES fragment(id)
     "fragment_schema_node_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (schema_node_id) REFERENCES schema_node(id)
 Referenced by:
     TABLE "fragment" CONSTRAINT "fragment_parent_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES  fragment(id)
     TABLE "relation" CONSTRAINT "relation_from_fragment_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY  (from_fragment_id) REFERENCES fragment(id)
     TABLE "relation" CONSTRAINT "relation_to_fragment_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY (to_fragment_id) REFERENCES fragment(id)
Suggetion : sounds a bit harsh. How about we call it "not-required"
